title: Chapter 1: The Trial's Husky Roar
content: The guards dragged Seraphina Vale into the Alpha's Hall by her elbows. Her bare feet scraped against the cold stone floor — a detail she noticed because noticing pain was easier than noticing the hundred pairs of wolf-gold eyes fixed on her from the shadows of the great hall.
The iron doors slammed shut behind her. The sound bounced off vaulted ceilings and died somewhere in the darkness above.
Sera had never been inside the Alpha's Hall before. Wolf-less weren't permitted.
"Kneel."

The guard's shove sent her sprawling. Her palms hit the stone, and a thin line of blood welled up where the rough edge had caught her skin. She stayed down. It was easier. She'd learned years ago that wolf-less who fought back got broken.

The throne sat at the far end of the hall, raised on a dais of black stone. Torches lined the walls, their flames throwing jumpy shadows across the assembled pack members. Nobles in the balconies. Warriors ringing the floor. And on the throne —
Kael Thornwood.

The Wolf King looked exactly like the stories said. Tall enough that even seated he commanded the room. Black hair pulled back from a face that had forgotten how to smile somewhere around age twelve. Eyes the color of molten gold, cold and still as frozen honey. He wore a dark military coat with silver buttons, and one scarred hand rested on the arm of his throne like it had been carved there.
He was terrifying. He was beautiful. And according to the whispers Sera had heard in the servant quarters, he had personally ordered her dragged here so he could pronounce her death.
*Or worse*, one of the kitchen girls had said with a shudder. *I heard he ripped out a traitor's throat with his teeth last winter. Didn't even transform first.*
Sera didn't look up. She stared at the stone between her fingers and tried to remember how to breathe.

"Seraphina Vale." The Beta's voice — Darius Stone, second-in-command — cut through the hall. "Daughter of House Vale, ward of the pack. You stand accused of theft from the Alpha's stores, conspiracy against the pack hierarchy, and — "

The list went on. Sera stopped listening after the word "theft." She hadn't stolen anything. She hadn't conspired against anyone. She was a wolf-less girl who spent her days scrubbing pots in the kitchen and her nights hiding from wolves who thought her existence was an insult to the species.
But wolf-less didn't get defenses. Wolf-less got sentences.
"— and the Alpha will now pronounce judgment," Darius finished.
The hall went silent. Not the everyday kind of silent — the kind where a hundred wolves stopped breathing at the same time.
Kael shifted on his throne. The torchlight caught his golden eyes, and for one dizzying second, Sera thought she saw something flicker in them. Something that didn't match the cold set of his jaw.
Then he spoke.

"Seraphina Vale." His voice was deep and even, utterly without warmth. "You are a stain upon this pack. A wolf-less parasite who offers nothing and takes everything. The evidence against you is overwhelming."
Sera's heart stopped.
"The sentence for these crimes is clear." Kael leaned forward slightly, and the torchlight carved his face into something beautiful and monstrous. "Tendon severance. Then exile into the Blackwood."

Someone in the noble balcony made a satisfied sound. Sera recognized that voice — Lady Isolde Vane. Her distant cousin. The woman who had smiled at her this morning and asked how she was enjoying the kitchen work.
*Tendon severance.* They were going to cut the cords in her legs so she could never walk properly again. Then throw her into a forest full of things that ate crippled wolf-less girls for sport.
Sera's vision swam. The stone floor blurred into grey water.
*I'm going to die.*
And then —
**— A VOICE THAT WASN'T HERS EXPLODED IN HER HEAD —**
*"WHAT?!"*
The mental shriek was so loud, so sudden, that Sera physically flinched. Her hands flew to her temples. The guards on either side of her tensed, probably thinking she was about to bolt.
*"NO. NO NO NO NO NO. WHOSE STUPID IDEA WAS THIS?!"*
The voice was... rich. Deep. Male. And absolutely, completely, spectacularly hysterical.
*"TENDONS?! EXILE?! WHO FRAMED MY WIFE?! I WILL FIND THEM. I WILL TEAR THEM APART. I WILL — "*
Sera's head snapped up.
The hall was still silent. Kael was still sitting on his throne with his face carved from ice. His mouth hadn't moved.
But the voice kept going.
*" — chew on their bones and use their skull as a CHEW TOY. DARIUS! DARIUS, TELL ME WHO DID THIS! Oh wait, I can't actually TALK to you because Mr. Cold-and-Heartless up here won't LET ME."*
Sera stared at Kael. Kael stared at nothing, golden eyes flat and empty.
*"She looks terrified. Look at her hands — she's BLEEDING. Someone MADE her bleed. I didn't even get to TOUCH her yet and someone already made her BLEED. This is the WORST DAY OF MY LIFE."*
The voice — it was coming from the Wolf King. From *inside* the Wolf King.
Sera stopped breathing for an entirely different reason.

*"Fine. FINE. If the cold-hearted idiot up front won't save her, then I will — I'll go on STRIKE. No more wolfing. No more transforming. No more being a cool scary Alpha. He can try being Alpha WITHOUT ME. See how long THAT lasts. I'll just sit here in his head and HOWL. For HOURS. DAYS. He'll go INSANE."*
Kael's jaw tightened almost imperceptibly.
*"Oh, NOW you notice me, you emotionally constipated — "*
"However."
The single word cut through the mental chaos like a knife. Kael's voice was still cold, still flat — but there was something new underneath it. Something that sounded almost... strained.
"In light of your bloodline connection to House Vane," Kael said, each word dragged out like it cost him physical pain, "I am willing to show... leniency."
A murmur rippled through the assembled wolves. Leniency? From the Wolf King who had once exiled his own uncle for questioning a hunting route?
*"YES! LENIENCY! That's my boy! I mean, me. I'm the boy. He's the — whatever, KEEP GOING."*

"You will not be exiled," Kael said, and his voice cracked on the last word — so quietly that only someone listening with supernatural attention would have caught it. "You will serve the Alpha's household directly. As my personal maid."
The murmur became an uproar.
Sera's mouth fell open. Her personal maid. *His* personal maid. The Wolf King — the cold, terrifying, throat-ripping Wolf King — had just commuted her death sentence to... domestic service?

*"PERSONAL MAID. She'll be in my ROOM. She'll touch my THINGS. She'll probably make my BED. OH MOON GODDESS, she's going to make my BED. Do I have clean sheets? Does my room smell? WHAT DOES MY ROOM SMELL LIKE?! DARIUS, WHY DIDN'T YOU TELL ME TO CLEAN MY ROOM?!"*
Sera clamped her hands over her mouth to stop herself from laughing — or screaming. She wasn't sure which.

"This judgment is final," Kael announced, rising from his throne. His face was still a mask of cold authority. His posture was still rigid with Alpha command. "Darius, have the girl escorted to the servant quarters. She begins at dawn."
He turned and strode from the hall without another glance.
The inner voice, trailing after him like a comet's tail of emotional chaos, faded with distance:

*"At dawn. She starts AT DAWN. That's like — that's only HOURS from now. Do I have time to get a HAIRCUT? Should I wear the coat with the silver buttons? THAT'S THIS COAT. I'm ALREADY wearing this coat. Okay, okay, calm down. Calm. DOWN. She's just a girl. A beautiful, silver-haired, grey-eyed girl with a face like moonlight and hands that bleed when someone's mean to her and — I'M GOING TO KILL WHOEVER MADE HER BLEED."*
The voice faded completely as Kael disappeared through the side door.
Sera knelt on the cold stone floor, her bleeding hands forgotten, her death sentence rewritten, and a single impossible truth burning behind her eyes:
She could hear the Wolf King's thoughts.
And the Wolf King — the terrifying, ruthless, kill-you-with-his-teeth Wolf King — was an absolute, catastrophic, tail-wagging disaster of a man.
Darius hauled her to her feet. "You're lucky, girl. He's never shown mercy before."
*You have no idea*, Sera thought.
And for the first time since she'd been dragged into this hall, she smiled.
